learning_outcomes.description: Practice good housekeeping Additional information about the unit Unit aim(s) Unit expiry date Details of the relationship between the unit and relevant National Occupational Standards (if appropriate) Details of the relationship between the unit and other standards or curricula (if appropriate) Assessment requirements specified by a sector or regulatory body (if appropriate) Endorsement of the unit by a sector or other appropriate body (if required) Location of the unit within the subject/sector classification system Name of the Organisation submitting the unit Guided Learning Hours

performance_criteria.description: Explain good housekeeping procedures before welding operations: • Ensure cleanliness of work environment • Proper positioning of tools, equipment and consumables • Ensure gangways are free from obstacles • Shield your work area • Proper illumination of the work area • Proper ventilation of the work area

Explain good housekeeping procedures during welding operations: • Ensure work environment is constantly clean • Ensure welding positioners are securely in place • Ensure work area is free from hot electrode stubs, work piece, water, oil/grease, paint. • Proper placement of electrical cables and gas hoses

Explain good housekeeping procedures after welding operations: • Assemble all tools, equipment and consumables after operations • Clean all tools, equipment and work area • Store tools and equipment appropriately • Switch off mains Nigerian Institute of Welding (NIW)

learning_outcomes.description: Understand Tungsten Inert Gas (TIG) Welding process

performance_criteria.description: Describe TIG Welding process
Identify the sign of TIG Welding process in a multi process welding machine
Identify the accessories for TIG Welding process such as Colet, ceramic cup, gas diffuser, Tail.
List the various consumables for TIG Welding process: • Gas: Argon and Helium • Filler rod
Select appropriate consumables for a given job.
List various techniques for TIG welding such as walking the cup and Lifting up
Apply techniques in 8.6 above
Identify types of Tungsten electrode: • 2% Thoriated (Red color) • 1.5% Lanthanated (Gold) • 2% Ceraiated (Grey) former Orange • 0.8% Zirconiated (White) • Pure green
Carry out appropriate preparation of tungsten electrode
Set up a TIG machine for a given task
Carry out TIG welding in fillet joints and positions 1F, 2F, 3F, 4F, Pipe on flange 5F, 4F,
Carry out TIG welding in butt joints and positions 1G, 2G, 3G and 4G
Describe FCAW Process
Identify the sign of FCAW process in a multi-process welding machine
List the various accessories for FCAW such as: • Contact tip • Nozzles • Gas diffuser
List the various consumables for FCAW process: • Gases: - CO 2 - 25% CO + 2 75% Argon (Ar) - 98% Argon (Ar) + 2% Oxygen (O ) 2 • Filler wires: - Gas shielding - Self- shielding • Ceramic backing
Set up a FCAW machine for a given task
Select appropriate accessories for FCAW
Carry out FCAW in fillet joints and positions 1F, 2F, 3F, 4F, Pipe on flange 5F, 4F,
Carry out FCAW in butt joints and positions 1G, 2G, 3G and 4G

Carry out MIG/MAG welding in fillet joints and positions: 1F, 2F, 3F, 4F, Pipe-on- flange 5F and 4F.
Carry out MIG/MAG welding in butt joints and positions: 1G, 2G, 3G and 4G
